items.q: Code a playable Pong game as a single self-contained HTML file (no external assets). Left paddle is the player, controlled with both the mouse and the ArrowUp/ArrowDown keys; right paddle is a simple AI. The ball bounces off the top/bottom walls and off paddles, and when a side scores the ball resets to the center. Show the two scores above the court in elements with ids "score-left" and "score-right". Dark background, clean minimal look. Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a playable Breakout game as a single self-contained HTML file. A paddle at the bottom controlled with the mouse and ArrowLeft/ArrowRight keys, a ball, and at least 4 rows of colorful bricks that disappear when hit. Show the score in an element with id "score" and remaining lives in an element with id "lives" (start with 3). The ball must launch automatically. Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a Minesweeper game as a single self-contained HTML file. A 9x9 grid with 10 hidden mines inside a container with id "board" (81 cells). Left click reveals a cell (numbers show adjacent mine counts, empty cells flood-reveal), right click plants a flag. Show remaining flags in an element with id "flags" and add a reset button with id "reset". Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a memory match card game as a single self-contained HTML file. A 4x4 grid of 16 face-down cards (8 emoji pairs, shuffled) inside a container with id "board". Clicking flips a card; two flipped non-matching cards flip back after a short delay, matches stay revealed. Count moves in an element with id "moves". Add a restart button with id "restart". Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a todo list app as a single self-contained HTML file. A text input with id "new-todo", an add button with id "add", and the list in an element with id "list". Each todo has a done-toggle and a delete button. Persist todos in localStorage so they survive a page reload. Pressing Enter in the input also adds the todo. Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a calculator as a single self-contained HTML file. A display element with id "display" and clickable buttons for digits 0-9, +, -, *, /, =, C (clear) and decimal point. It must handle chained operations (e.g. 2 + 3 * 4 pressed sequentially evaluating left-to-right or with precedence — your choice, but consistent) and division by zero without crashing. Nice grid layout. Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a countdown timer as a single self-contained HTML file. A number input with id "minutes" to set minutes, a start button with id "start", a pause button with id "pause", a reset button with id "reset", and the remaining time shown as MM:SS in an element with id "time". When it reaches 00:00 the page should indicate time is up (message or visual change). Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a bouncing balls physics simulation as a single self-contained HTML file. A full-window canvas starts with 15 balls of random colors, sizes and velocities that bounce off the window edges with slight gravity. Clicking the canvas adds a new ball at the click position. Show the current ball count in an element with id "count". Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a particle fountain as a single self-contained HTML file. A full-window dark canvas; while the mouse button is held down, colorful particles continuously spray from the cursor position, rise, then fall with gravity and fade out. Particles must be removed once invisible so it never slows down. Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a sortable data table as a single self-contained HTML file. A table with id "tbl" pre-filled with 8 rows of plausible sample people (columns: Name, Age, City). Clicking a column header sorts the rows by that column, clicking again reverses the order, and the active header shows a ▲/▼ indicator. Rows must visually alternate background colors. Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a drawing pad as a single self-contained HTML file. A white canvas where dragging the mouse draws smooth lines, a color picker input with id "color", a brush size range input with id "size", and a clear button with id "clear". The cursor must draw exactly under the pointer even though the canvas has a toolbar above it. Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.

Code a whack-a-mole game as a single self-contained HTML file. A 3x3 grid of holes inside a container with id "board", a start button with id "start", the score in an element with id "score" and a 30-second countdown in an element with id "timer". After pressing start, a mole pops up in a random hole roughly every 700ms; clicking the mole increments the score and hides it. Reply with one complete HTML file in a ```html code block.
